title: Crispy Umami Algerian Chicken Tagine
description: A savory, crispy chicken tagine bursting with umami from a fragrant spice blend, perfect for a quick low‑calorie weeknight dinner.

ingredients.name: Mixed spice powder (cumin 1 tsp, coriander 1 tsp, paprika 1 tsp, smoked paprika 1/2 tsp, garlic powder 1/2 tsp, onion powder 1/2 tsp, cayenne 1/4 tsp, salt 1/2 tsp)

instructions: Pat chicken dry and sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a Dutch oven over medium‑high. Sear chicken pieces until golden brown on all sides, about 3–4 minutes per batch; set aside.
Add remaining 1 tbsp olive oil to the pot, sauté sliced onion until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in the mixed spice powder and toast for 1 minute to release aromas.
Add cherry tomato halves, cook until they soften, about 2 minutes.
Return chicken to the pot, pour in chicken broth and lemon juice, and bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 20 minutes until chicken is tender and sauce thickens slightly.
Remove the lid, increase heat to medium, and let the sauce reduce until it coats the chicken lightly, about 5 minutes.
Stir in chopped parsley, adjust seasoning with salt or pepper if needed, and serve hot over couscous or cauliflower rice.
